tool_design.role_in_intervention: Accuracy-control record ensuring the probe used in B001 can be trusted.
tool_design.usage_moment: Monthly and whenever the probe is dropped or questioned
tool_design.expected_output: Calibration record with variance, pass/fail status, and next due date

failure_warnings.warning: Entries are logged late or inconsistently.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: Patterns cannot be reviewed or escalated.

failure_warnings.warning: Tracker is updated but never used to decide action.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: Information is stored but not acted on.

content:
# Probe Calibration Record & Accuracy Tracker **Tool ID:** T001-02 **Block:** B001 (Daily Temperature Monitoring) **Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety) **Level:** L2 **Format:** Tracker **Role:** Manager / Food Safety Lead **Time Required:** 10 minutes per calibration **Version:** 2.0 --- ## What This Tool Is For Use this tracker to prove that your thermometer can be trusted before staff use it to make food safety decisions. A daily log is only as good as the probe behind it. This tool makes calibration visible, repeatable, and reviewable. Use it: - monthly as standard - after the probe is dropped - after battery change - when readings look suspicious - before an EHO visit if the device history is unclear --- ## Pass Standard | Check Method | Target Reading | Pass Window | Fail Action | |---|---|---|---| | Ice-water test | `0C` | `-1C` to `+1C` | Re-test once. If still outside range, remove from use and replace or recalibrate. | **Important:** If the probe fails calibration, stop using it for `T001-01` until the issue is resolved. --- ## Calibration Method ### Ice-water method
  • Fill a cup with crushed ice and a small amount of clean water.
  • Stir for 15 seconds so the slush is evenly cold.
  • Insert the probe tip into the middle without touching sides or base.
  • Wait until the reading stabilises.
  • Record the result and the variance from `0C`.
  • If out of range, repeat once and decide whether the probe stays in service.
--- ## Calibration Tracker **Probe ID / Label:** ____________________ **Storage location:** ____________________ **Owned by venue / manager:** ____________________ | Date | Probe ID | Test Method | Target | Actual Reading | Variance | Pass / Fail | Action Taken | Checked By | Next Due | |---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---| | | | Ice-water | `0C` | | | | | | | | | | Ice-water | `0C` | | | | | | | | | | Ice-water | `0C` | | | | | | | | | | Ice-water | `0C` | | | | | | | | | | Ice-water | `0C` | | | | | | | | | | Ice-water | `0C` | | | | | | | --- ## Accuracy Trend Check Use this section when the probe keeps drifting close to the limit. | Month | Result Pattern | Risk Level | Decision | |---|---|---|---| | | Stable within `0.5C` | Low | Keep in service | | | Repeatedly near `+/-1C` | Medium | Monitor weekly until stable | | | Outside window twice in a row | High | Replace or professionally recalibrate | --- ## Replacement Or Escalation Record | Date | Issue Found | Temporary Cover Used? | Replacement Ordered? | Closed By | |---|---|---|---|---| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | --- ## Common Failure Points - Probe stored wet or dirty between uses - Probe dropped during service and put back without re-checking accuracy - Team assumes digital means accurate - No back-up thermometer on site when the main one fails --- ## Connected Resources **Used with:** - T001-01 - Daily Temperature Log & Check Sheet - T001-03 - Temperature Excursion Trigger Log - T001-05 - Temperature Monitoring SOP & Team Sign-Off **Related blocks:** - B003 - Temperature Excursion Response --- *Tool Version 2.0 - because an accurate record starts with an accurate probe.*
